sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 48.8g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for seeds, the fat profile is one of the biggest reasons they earn their place.
sections.subtitleText: 48.8g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for seeds, the fat profile is one of the biggest reasons they earn their place.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 8.7g of carbs. fibre is where things start to fall off. for a seed, that is a huge win.
sections.subtitleText: 8.7g of carbs. fibre is where things start to fall off. for a seed, that is a huge win.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 31.6g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein is a bonus, but not enough by itself.
sections.subtitleText: 31.6g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein is a bonus, but not enough by itself.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: everything else here is pretty flat.
sections.subtitleText: everything else here is pretty flat.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: magnesium at 175% daily value, zinc at 66% daily value, iron at 44% daily value stand out most. that is major mineral support.
sections.subtitleText: magnesium at 175% daily value, zinc at 66% daily value, iron at 44% daily value stand out most. that is major mineral support.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: the upsides first: tiny food with serious nutrient density because seeds often overperform for their size. easy to sprinkle into meals because that practical versatility matters. usually brings decent fat support because that is one of the category strengths.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: the drawbacks next: calorie density still stacks up fast because tiny foods can still overshoot fast. real serving sizes are often small because some strong paper numbers are hard to cash out in practice. texture or prep can be a barrier because not everyone uses seeds consistently.

closing.summary: Hemp Seeds has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best as a support food that boosts meals instead of carrying them alone.
closing.useCaseNote: Best as a support food that boosts meals instead of carrying them alone.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?
